notes: Cross-check source; use ACS for TAM, CPS for full-year uninsured stress test (P04)

title: Key Facts about the Uninsured Population
claim_supported: 26.7M people ages 0-64 uninsured in 2024 (9.8% rate); adults 19-64 uninsured rate 11.3%; children under 19 rate 5.9%; 42% of uninsured under 65 in non-expansion states
supporting_excerpt: The total number of people ages 0-64 without health coverage increased by more than 1.3 million to 26.7 million in 2024, and the uninsured rate for the population under age 65 increased from 9.5% to 9.8%.
limitations: KFF analysis of ACS; Medicare-eligible 65+ excluded from under-65 counts
notes: Under-65 uninsured anchor; derives ~22M working-age uninsured via residual (uninsured_population_sizing.md)

title: Comparing Federal Government Surveys That Count the Uninsured: 2025
claim_supported: ACS 2024: 27.5M uninsured, 8.2% point-in-time; reconciles ACS/CPS/NHIS/BRFSS/MEPS national estimates and methodology differences
limitations: Synthesis document; ACS count from SHADAC analysis of Census Table S2701
notes: Use when explaining why surveys disagree; 27.5M = all-ages point-in-time (uninsured_population_sizing.md §1)

title: Health insurance coverage: Early release of estimates from the National Health Interview Survey, 2024
claim_supported: 27.2M all ages uninsured at interview (8.2%); 23.1M adults 18-64 uninsured (11.6%) in 2024
supporting_excerpt: In 2024, 27.2 million people of all ages (8.2%) were uninsured at the time of interview... 23.1 million adults ages 18-64 (11.6%) were uninsured.
limitations: NHIS point-in-time; slightly different age bands than ACS 19-64
notes: Cross-check for working-age uninsured count (~23M)

title: NSDUH 2024 Detailed Tables Section 6 — Mental Illness by Insurance Status
claim_supported: Uninsured adults 18+ with AMI: ~5.9M (2024); uninsured with SMI: ~1.6M; 24.7% of uninsured with AMI received MH treatment in past year
supporting_excerpt: No Coverage | 4,958b | 5,928 [thousands, adults 18+ with AMI by insurance status, Table 6.3A]
limitations: AMI/SMI are model-predicted not diagnosed; insurance categories non-mutually-exclusive; 2024 treatment questions not comparable to prior years
notes: Psychiatry-relevant uninsured TAM anchor T2/T3/T4 (uninsured_population_sizing.md §2)

title: NSDUH 2024 Detailed Tables Section 6.8B — AMI Prevalence Among Adults 18+
claim_supported: 23.4% of US adults 18+ had any mental illness in past year (2024); validates ~5.9M uninsured AMI as ~23% of ~23M uninsured adults
supporting_excerpt: TOTAL | 22.8 | 23.4 [percent with any mental illness, adults 18+, 2023 and 2024]
limitations: Prevalence model not clinical census
notes: Cross-check for T2 uninsured AMI sizing
